Academic & Independent Work

Research

A collection of coursework and independent projects spanning sports analytics, statistical modeling, simulation, and data visualization.

Coursework Projects
Simulation
R
Yahtzee Score Card Strategy Simulator
Monte Carlo simulation exploring whether prioritizing the top half vs. bottom half of a Yahtzee scorecard produces better expected outcomes across thousands of simulated games.
Monte Carlo Simulation Game Theory
↗ GitHub ↓ Report PDF
Sports Analytics
Python + R
Impact of Weather on NFL Performance
Analyzed how weather conditions affect NFL performance metrics across three stakeholder lenses — coaches, fantasy football players, and sports bettors — using feature selection, random forest, and regression models.
Random Forest Regression Feature Selection Visualization
↗ GitHub ↓ Report PDF
Regression Analysis
Python + R
Austin Housing Market Regression
Built and compared linear regression, random forest, and gradient boosting models to assess home values across the Austin area, with supporting data visualizations and model validation.
Linear Regression Random Forest Gradient Boost Visualization
↗ GitHub ↓ Report PDF
Analytics
Report Only
The Indiana Department of Corrections and Reduction of Violent Attacks
Proposed an analytical framework to help the Indiana Department of Corrections reduce a surge in inmate attacks on staff. Identified limitations in the existing tool and proposed a multi-model solution to more accurately predict and communicate violence risk.
Logistic Regression Survival Analysis Community Detection Risk Assessment
↓ Report PDF
